topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. I stared at the screen, the pixels blurring into an unreadable mess. "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise party for Sarah has been cancelled." My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscles ache. A slow, burning sensation started in my chest, spreading outwards. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. I didn't move for a full minute, just breathing in short, ragged gasps. Every nerve ending felt frayed, sparking with a sharp, unpleasant tension. I stood up, pacing the cramped living room, my movements jerky and sharp. I wanted to shout, to demand an explanation, but the words caught in my throat, replaced by a cold, hard knot of resentment twisting in my gut.

[story 2]

I reread the message three times, each reading bringing a fresh wave of icy fury. This wasn't just disappointing; it felt like a deliberate snub. My hands clenched into fists so tight my knuckles turned white. I walked over to the window, looking out at the indifferent streetlights. The air felt thick and suffocating. A low growl vibrated in my throat, a sound I barely recognized. I turned back to the computer, my eyes narrowed to slits. I typed a reply, my fingers moving with deliberate, aggressive precision, each keystroke laced with sharp, controlled venom. I hit send before I could second-guess the harshness of the language. The satisfaction was fleeting, quickly replaced by a hollow, vibrating emptiness.

[story 3]

The phone call was short, clipped, and utterly devoid of warmth. When the confirmation came—the cancellation—I felt a sudden, violent surge of anger that made my vision swim momentarily. I gripped the phone so tightly I worried the plastic would crack. I could feel my cheeks flushing hot, a furious heat rising from my core. "Unforeseen circumstances?" I managed to spit the words out, my voice dangerously level, each syllable weighted with undisguised displeasure. I didn't wait for an answer. I ended the call abruptly, tossing the device onto the counter with unnecessary force. I walked to the kitchen, staring into the sink, my breath coming in shallow, rapid bursts. The silence that followed was deafening, filled only with the drumming pulse of my own rising irritation.
